experiment: F-STR1 EAD regression root cause analysis
method: Per-session EAD decomposition of -32.7pp regression (L-741). Tested pace hypothesis vs initialization hypothesis. Checked close_lane.py enforcement mechanism.
expect: EAD regression driven by session pace (lanes/session overloading compliance)
actual: Pace hypothesis FALSIFIED. Root cause: initialization effect (S380) + diff-as-warning enforcement gap
diff: Expected pace-driven, found tool-enforcement-driven. S381 (11 lanes) had 90% EAD; S380 (3 lanes) had 33%.

initialization_effect.description: S380 (3 lanes, 33% EAD) is the primary driver. First session after value_density integration focused on code change, not compliance. Old close_lane.py only enforced EAD if lane had expect= field set.

diff_warning_gap.description: close_lane.py requires --actual (ERROR) but --diff is WARNING only. 3/6 non-compliant lanes had actual but no diff. These pass the error gate but fail EAD.
diff_warning_gap.fix: Upgrade --diff from WARNING to ERROR for MERGED lanes

pace_hypothesis_falsified.description: S381 (11 lanes) achieved 90% EAD (merged-only). S374 baseline (9 lanes) had 89% EAD. High lane volume does NOT cause EAD failure.

remediation: Upgrade --diff to ERROR in close_lane.py for MERGED lanes
S380 initialization effect is one-time — no recurring fix needed
Value_density dispatch itself is NOT the cause of EAD regression
